tasks.title: Remediate Sensitive Credential Storage in ConfigMap
tasks.description: The 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap (created in Task 9) currently stores sensitive credentials such as S3 access keys and database connection strings. ConfigMaps are not suitable for sensitive data as they are not encrypted at rest by default and have broader access. This task involves refactoring the storage of these credentials into Kubernetes Secrets and ensuring applications consume them securely.
subtasks: Modify the 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap to remove all sensitive credentials (S3 keys, database connection strings if they contain credentials).
Create new Kubernetes Secrets for database connection strings (e.g., 'sigma1-postgres-credentials', 'sigma1-valkey-credentials') in the 'sigma1' namespace, containing only the necessary credentials.

Update application deployment manifests (not in scope for this plan, but note for future) to consume S3 credentials from 'sigma1-s3-credentials' Secret (Task 8) and database credentials from the newly created Secrets, via environment variables or mounted volumes, instead of the ConfigMap.
Ensure the 'CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_HOSTNAME' and 'S3_BUCKET_NAME' remain in the ConfigMap as they are not sensitive.
Document the secure method for credential consumption for future application deployments.
acceptance_criteria: The 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap in the 'sigma1' namespace no longer contains 'S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID', 'S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY', or full database connection strings with credentials.
New Kubernetes Secrets are created for database credentials (e.g., 'sigma1-postgres-credentials', 'sigma1-valkey-credentials') in the 'sigma1' namespace.
Verification that applications (once deployed) can successfully retrieve and use credentials from the appropriate Secrets.

tasks.title: Secure Kubernetes API Server Access via Cloudflare Tunnel
tasks.description: Task 5 configured a Cloudflare Tunnel to expose the Kubernetes API server publicly. This poses a significant security risk by increasing the attack surface of the cluster's control plane. This task involves removing direct public exposure and implementing a more secure access method.
subtasks: Modify the Cloudflare Tunnel configuration (created in Task 5) to remove the ingress rule pointing to the Kubernetes API server.
Implement Cloudflare Access policies for the tunnel's public hostname, requiring strong authentication (e.g., MFA, specific identity provider) for any access.
Alternatively, if direct public access is not strictly required, remove the tunnel configuration entirely and recommend a VPN or bastion host for remote access.
Update documentation regarding secure remote access methods for the Kubernetes API.
acceptance_criteria: The Cloudflare Tunnel's public hostname no longer directly exposes the Kubernetes API server without authentication.
If the tunnel remains, Cloudflare Access policies are configured and enforced, requiring authentication to reach the API server endpoint.
Attempting to access the public hostname without proper authentication (or VPN/bastion) results in a denied access or connection error.

tasks.title: Implement Pod Security Contexts for Database Deployments
tasks.description: The PostgreSQL and Valkey database deployments (from Tasks 6 and 7) are missing Pod Security Contexts, which can lead to elevated privileges and increased impact in case of a container breakout. This task involves adding appropriate security contexts to these deployments.
subtasks: Modify the CloudNative-PG 'Cluster' Custom Resource for 'sigma1-postgres' to include a 'securityContext' for the PostgreSQL pods.
Ensure the security context specifies 'runAsNonRoot: true', 'readOnlyRootFilesystem: true', and drops unnecessary capabilities (e.g., 'NET_RAW', 'SYS_CHROOT').
Modify the Redis/Valkey 'Redis' (or equivalent) Custom Resource for 'sigma1-valkey' to include a 'securityContext' for the Valkey pods.
Ensure the security context specifies 'runAsNonRoot: true', 'readOnlyRootFilesystem: true', and drops unnecessary capabilities.
Verify that the database pods restart successfully with the new security contexts applied.
acceptance_criteria: The 'sigma1-postgres' pods are running with 'runAsNonRoot: true' and dropped capabilities as verified by 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n databases'.
The 'sigma1-valkey' pods are running with 'runAsNonRoot: true' and dropped capabilities as verified by 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n databases'.
Database functionality (connection, read/write operations) remains unaffected after applying security contexts.
